title: OJS Durable Execution Checkpoint
description: Schema for OJS durable execution checkpoints. Checkpoints enable jobs to save intermediate state for resumability after failures, preemptions, or restarts. Each job has at most one checkpoint; the latest save overwrites the previous.

checkpointRequest.title: Checkpoint Save Request
checkpointRequest.description: Request body for POST /ojs/v1/jobs/{id}/checkpoint. Saves or overwrites the checkpoint for the given job.

state.title: Checkpoint State
state.description: Any valid JSON value representing the job's intermediate state. MUST NOT exceed 1 MB (1,048,576 bytes) when serialized.

sequence.title: Sequence Number
sequence.description: Monotonically increasing checkpoint counter. If provided, the backend MUST reject the save if the sequence is not strictly greater than the stored sequence (optimistic concurrency).

checkpointResponse.title: Checkpoint Response
checkpointResponse.description: Response body for GET /ojs/v1/jobs/{id}/checkpoint and POST /ojs/v1/jobs/{id}/checkpoint.

job_id.title: Job ID
job_id.description: The job this checkpoint belongs to.

state.title: Checkpoint State
state.description: The stored checkpoint state. Any valid JSON value.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent checkpoint save in RFC 3339 format.
